title: Partition the design into voltage domains
description: Group logic by performance need into separate voltage domains and place level shifters on every crossing.
body: Use when parts of a design can run slower at a lower voltage to save power. Do not let signals cross between voltage domains without level shifters, since a low-voltage output may not drive a high-voltage input reliably. Actions: assign blocks to domains by performance need, insert level shifters on each crossing, and verify the power-intent file declares them. Validation signals include every domain crossing carrying a level shifter and timing closing at each domain's voltage. Risk boundary: a missing level shifter causes unreliable logic levels, so flag any undeclared voltage crossing before sign-off.
